summary: Both clone ranges contain the same `type !== X && ... && type !== 'undefined'` guard pattern at their first and last lines. I extract that into a single `isAllowedType` helper that allows `'undefined'` plus a variadic list of permitted typeof strings, and rewrite the two guard lines in each range to call it while leaving the intervening lines byte-identical.
rationale: The repeated logic is a typeof guard that always permits `'undefined'` plus a fixed set of types; `isAllowedType` captures it variadically. Only the two guard expressions per range change, so the `const type = ...` lines stay used and observable behavior is identical.
